forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,46 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: d080
+title: 'Commands: document cross-cutting vs type-specific rule + add YAML hygiene test'
+---
+## What
+
+Establish the rule that the rest of this refactor enforces. Today there is no documented convention for what belongs in `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/entity.yaml` versus a type-specific command YAML (`task.yaml`, `tag.yaml`, …) versus a per-entity schema (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/entities/*.yaml`). The ambiguity is the root cause of the bugs addressed by the rest of this plan.
+
+### Rule to encode
+
+- **Command declarations** (full contract: `id`, `name`, `params`, `undoable`) live in `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/*.yaml`, split by noun:
+  - `entity.yaml` — cross-cutting commands that apply to any entity by target moniker: `entity.add`, `entity.delete`, `entity.archive`, `entity.unarchive`, `entity.cut`, `entity.copy`, `entity.paste`, `entity.update_field`. No `scope:` pinning.
+  - `task.yaml`, `tag.yaml`, `project.yaml`, `column.yaml`, `attachment.yaml`, `perspective.yaml`, etc. — type-specific commands. (Some of these files do not exist yet — later cards create them.)
+- **Per-entity schemas** (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/entities/*.yaml`) list *which* commands apply to an entity, plus *overlay* metadata (`context_menu`, `keys`, `menu`, templated `name`). Entries never re-declare `params`, `undoable`, or scope pins — those live with the command declaration.
+
+### Files to touch
+
+- `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/entity.yaml` — add a header comment explaining the rule and the cross-cutting contract.
+- `/Users/wballard/.claude/projects/-Users-wballard-github-swissarmyhammer-swissarmyhammer/memory/` — add a new feedback or reference memory `feedback_command_organization.md` capturing the rule as a convention, and link it from `MEMORY.md`.
+- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/scope_commands.rs` (tests module) — add a YAML-hygiene test that loads every file in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/entities/*.yaml` and asserts: for any `commands:` entry whose `id` starts with `entity.`, the entry has no `params` key, no `undoable` key, and no `scope` key (overlay-only).
+
+### Subtasks
+
+- [ ] Write the rule as a comment header in `entity.yaml`.
+- [ ] Write the memory file and index it from `MEMORY.md`.
+- [ ] Add the hygiene test. It MUST fail on the current branch — that failure is what the rest of this plan drives to green.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/entity.yaml` opens with a comment block stating the rule.
+- [ ] `MEMORY.md` indexes a new `feedback_command_organization.md` memory.
+- [ ] The hygiene test exists and **fails on this branch** because `task.yaml`, `tag.yaml`, and `project.yaml` all still re-declare cross-cutting commands with `params` / `name` / `scope` fields.
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] Add `yaml_hygiene_entity_schemas_overlay_only` in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/scope_commands.rs` tests module. Load every `builtin/entities/*.yaml` via the existing loader, iterate commands, and fail with a clear list of offenders.
+- [ ] Run command: `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-kanban yaml_hygiene_entity_schemas_overlay_only` — expect FAIL on this branch, expect PASS after the rest of the plan lands.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd` — the hygiene test is the RED step for the whole plan. Commit it failing; later cards make it green.
+
+#commands
\ No newline at end of file

changes.value: Commands: document cross-cutting vs type-specific rule + add YAML hygiene test

## What

Establish the rule that the rest of this refactor enforces. Today there is no documented convention for what belongs in `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/entity.yaml` versus a type-specific command YAML (`task.yaml`, `tag.yaml`, …) versus a per-entity schema (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/entities/*.yaml`). The ambiguity is the root cause of the bugs addressed by the rest of this plan.

### Rule to encode

- **Command declarations** (full contract: `id`, `name`, `params`, `undoable`) live in `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/*.yaml`, split by noun:
  - `entity.yaml` — cross-cutting commands that apply to any entity by target moniker: `entity.add`, `entity.delete`, `entity.archive`, `entity.unarchive`, `entity.cut`, `entity.copy`, `entity.paste`, `entity.update_field`. No `scope:` pinning.
  - `task.yaml`, `tag.yaml`, `project.yaml`, `column.yaml`, `attachment.yaml`, `perspective.yaml`, etc. — type-specific commands. (Some of these files do not exist yet — later cards create them.)
- **Per-entity schemas** (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/entities/*.yaml`) list *which* commands apply to an entity, plus *overlay* metadata (`context_menu`, `keys`, `menu`, templated `name`). Entries never re-declare `params`, `undoable`, or scope pins — those live with the command declaration.

### Files to touch

- `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/entity.yaml` — add a header comment explaining the rule and the cross-cutting contract.
- `/Users/wballard/.claude/projects/-Users-wballard-github-swissarmyhammer-swissarmyhammer/memory/` — add a new feedback or reference memory `feedback_command_organization.md` capturing the rule as a convention, and link it from `MEMORY.md`.
- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/scope_commands.rs` (tests module) — add a YAML-hygiene test that loads every file in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/entities/*.yaml` and asserts: for any `commands:` entry whose `id` starts with `entity.`, the entry has no `params` key, no `undoable` key, and no `scope` key (overlay-only).

### Subtasks

- [ ] Write the rule as a comment header in `entity.yaml`.
- [ ] Write the memory file and index it from `MEMORY.md`.
- [ ] Add the hygiene test. It MUST fail on the current branch — that failure is what the rest of this plan drives to green.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/entity.yaml` opens with a comment block stating the rule.
- [ ] `MEMORY.md` indexes a new `feedback_command_organization.md` memory.
- [ ] The hygiene test exists and **fails on this branch** because `task.yaml`, `tag.yaml`, and `project.yaml` all still re-declare cross-cutting commands with `params` / `name` / `scope` fields.

## Tests

- [ ] Add `yaml_hygiene_entity_schemas_overlay_only` in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/scope_commands.rs` tests module. Load every `builtin/entities/*.yaml` via the existing loader, iterate commands, and fail with a clear list of offenders.
- [ ] Run command: `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-kanban yaml_hygiene_entity_schemas_overlay_only` — expect FAIL on this branch, expect PASS after the rest of the plan lands.

## Workflow

- Use `/tdd` — the hygiene test is the RED step for the whole plan. Commit it failing; later cards make it green.

#commands

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -3,44 +3,49 @@
 - claude-code
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: d080
-title: 'Commands: document cross-cutting vs type-specific rule + add YAML hygiene test'
+title: 'Commands: document cross-cutting rule + YAML hygiene test (no cross-cutting in entity schemas)'
 ---
 ## What

-Establish the rule that the rest of this refactor enforces. Today there is no documented convention for what belongs in `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/entity.yaml` versus a type-specific command YAML (`task.yaml`, `tag.yaml`, …) versus a per-entity schema (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/entities/*.yaml`). The ambiguity is the root cause of the bugs addressed by the rest of this plan.
+Establish the rule the rest of this plan enforces. Today there is no documented convention for what belongs in `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/entity.yaml` vs. a type-specific command YAML vs. a per-entity schema (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/entities/*.yaml`). Duplicate `ui.inspect` blocks copy-pasted across six entity YAMLs, redeclared `entity.*` commands inside `task.yaml`/`tag.yaml`/`project.yaml`, and a broken `project.delete` all trace back to this ambiguity.

 ### Rule to encode

-- **Command declarations** (full contract: `id`, `name`, `params`, `undoable`) live in `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/*.yaml`, split by noun:
-  - `entity.yaml` — cross-cutting commands that apply to any entity by target moniker: `entity.add`, `entity.delete`, `entity.archive`, `entity.unarchive`, `entity.cut`, `entity.copy`, `entity.paste`, `entity.update_field`. No `scope:` pinning.
-  - `task.yaml`, `tag.yaml`, `project.yaml`, `column.yaml`, `attachment.yaml`, `perspective.yaml`, etc. — type-specific commands. (Some of these files do not exist yet — later cards create them.)
-- **Per-entity schemas** (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/entities/*.yaml`) list *which* commands apply to an entity, plus *overlay* metadata (`context_menu`, `keys`, `menu`, templated `name`). Entries never re-declare `params`, `undoable`, or scope pins — those live with the command declaration.
+- **Command declarations** (full contract: `id`, `name`, `params`, `undoable`, `keys`, `context_menu`, and a new `per_entity` flag) live in `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/*.yaml`, split by noun:
+  - `entity.yaml` — cross-cutting commands that apply to any entity by target moniker: `entity.add`, `entity.delete`, `entity.archive`, `entity.unarchive`, `entity.cut`, `entity.copy`, `entity.paste`, `entity.update_field`. No `scope:` pinning. All marked `per_entity: true`.
+  - `ui.yaml` — UI commands, including the cross-cutting `ui.inspect` (also `per_entity: true`).
+  - `task.yaml`, `tag.yaml`, `project.yaml`, `column.yaml`, `attachment.yaml`, `perspective.yaml` — type-specific commands only.
+- **Per-entity schemas** (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/entities/*.yaml`) list ONLY type-specific command references with overlay metadata (`context_menu`, `keys`). Cross-cutting commands are NOT listed — they auto-emit via the registry flag. If an entity must opt *out* of a cross-cutting command (e.g. attachment does not archive), the Rust `available()` impl on that command handles it.
+
+### Hygiene test
+
+The test scans every `swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/entities/*.yaml` and fails if any entry's `id` matches a known cross-cutting command (`ui.inspect`, `entity.delete`, `entity.archive`, `entity.unarchive`, `entity.copy`, `entity.cut`, `entity.paste`, `entity.add`, `entity.update_field`). Those commands must not appear in entity schemas at all — they emit via the cross-cutting mechanism.

 ### Files to touch

-- `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/entity.yaml` — add a header comment explaining the rule and the cross-cutting contract.
-- `/Users/wballard/.claude/projects/-Users-wballard-github-swissarmyhammer-swissarmyhammer/memory/` — add a new feedback or reference memory `feedback_command_organization.md` capturing the rule as a convention, and link it from `MEMORY.md`.
-- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/scope_commands.rs` (tests module) — add a YAML-hygiene test that loads every file in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/entities/*.yaml` and asserts: for any `commands:` entry whose `id` starts with `entity.`, the entry has no `params` key, no `undoable` key, and no `scope` key (overlay-only).
+- `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/entity.yaml` — header comment block explaining the rule and listing cross-cutting IDs.
+- `/Users/wballard/.claude/projects/-Users-wballard-github-swissarmyhammer-swissarmyhammer/memory/` — add `feedback_command_organization.md` memory and index from `MEMORY.md`.
+- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/scope_commands.rs` (tests module) — add `yaml_hygiene_no_cross_cutting_in_entity_schemas`.

 ### Subtasks

-- [ ] Write the rule as a comment header in `entity.yaml`.
-- [ ] Write the memory file and index it from `MEMORY.md`.
-- [ ] Add the hygiene test. It MUST fail on the current branch — that failure is what the rest of this plan drives to green.
+- [ ] Write rule as a header comment in `entity.yaml` with the cross-cutting ID list.
+- [ ] Write the feedback memory file and link from `MEMORY.md`.
+- [ ] Add the hygiene test. It MUST fail on the current branch (six entity YAMLs list `ui.inspect`; task/tag/project YAMLs list more cross-cutting commands).

 ## Acceptance Criteria

-- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/entity.yaml` opens with a comment block stating the rule.
+- [ ] `entity.yaml` opens with the rule-comment header naming the cross-cutting IDs.
 - [ ] `MEMORY.md` indexes a new `feedback_command_organization.md` memory.
-- [ ] The hygiene test exists and **fails on this branch** because `task.yaml`, `tag.yaml`, and `project.yaml` all still re-declare cross-cutting commands with `params` / `name` / `scope` fields.
+- [ ] Hygiene test exists and **fails on this branch** — the failure drives the rest of the plan.

 ## Tests

-- [ ] Add `yaml_hygiene_entity_schemas_overlay_only` in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/scope_commands.rs` tests module. Load every `builtin/entities/*.yaml` via the existing loader, iterate commands, and fail with a clear list of offenders.
-- [ ] Run command: `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-kanban yaml_hygiene_entity_schemas_overlay_only` — expect FAIL on this branch, expect PASS after the rest of the plan lands.
+- [ ] Add `yaml_hygiene_no_cross_cutting_in_entity_schemas` in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/scope_commands.rs` tests module. Load every `builtin/entities/*.yaml`, iterate `commands:` entries, fail listing every entity/command pair that violates the rule.
+- [ ] Run command: `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-kanban yaml_hygiene_no_cross_cutting_in_entity_schemas` — expect FAIL on this branch, expect PASS after the plan lands.

 ## Workflow

-- Use `/tdd` — the hygiene test is the RED step for the whole plan. Commit it failing; later cards make it green.
+- Use `/tdd` — the hygiene test is the RED step for the whole plan. Commit it failing; later cards turn it green.

+  - `entity.yaml` — cross-cutting commands that apply to any entity by target moniker: `entity.add`, `entity.delete`, `entity.archive`, `entity.unarchive`, `entity.cut`, `entity.copy`, `entity.paste`, `entity.update_field`. No `scope:` pinning. All marked `per_entity: true`.
+  - `ui.yaml` — UI commands, including the cross-cutting `ui.inspect` (also `per_entity: true`).
+  - `task.yaml`, `tag.yaml`, `project.yaml`, `column.yaml`, `attachment.yaml`, `perspective.yaml` — type-specific commands only.
+- **Per-entity schemas** (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/entities/*.yaml`) list ONLY type-specific command references with overlay metadata (`context_menu`, `keys`). Cross-cutting commands are NOT listed — they auto-emit via the registry flag. If an entity must opt *out* of a cross-cutting command (e.g. attachment does not archive), the Rust `available()` impl on that command handles it.
+
+### Hygiene test
+
+The test scans every `swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/entities/*.yaml` and fails if any entry's `id` matches a known cross-cutting command (`ui.inspect`, `entity.delete`, `entity.archive`, `entity.unarchive`, `entity.copy`, `entity.cut`, `entity.paste`, `entity.add`, `entity.update_field`). Those commands must not appear in entity schemas at all — they emit via the cross-cutting mechanism.

 ### Files to touch

-- `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/entity.yaml` — add a header comment explaining the rule and the cross-cutting contract.
-- `/Users/wballard/.claude/projects/-Users-wballard-github-swissarmyhammer-swissarmyhammer/memory/` — add a new feedback or reference memory `feedback_command_organization.md` capturing the rule as a convention, and link it from `MEMORY.md`.
-- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/scope_commands.rs` (tests module) — add a YAML-hygiene test that loads every file in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/entities/*.yaml` and asserts: for any `commands:` entry whose `id` starts with `entity.`, the entry has no `params` key, no `undoable` key, and no `scope` key (overlay-only).
+- `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/entity.yaml` — header comment block explaining the rule and listing cross-cutting IDs.
+- `/Users/wballard/.claude/projects/-Users-wballard-github-swissarmyhammer-swissarmyhammer/memory/` — add `feedback_command_organization.md` memory and index from `MEMORY.md`.
+- `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/scope_commands.rs` (tests module) — add `yaml_hygiene_no_cross_cutting_in_entity_schemas`.

 ### Subtasks

-- [ ] Write the memory file and index it from `MEMORY.md`.
-- [ ] Add the hygiene test. It MUST fail on the current branch — that failure is what the rest of this plan drives to green.
+- [ ] Write rule as a header comment in `entity.yaml` with the cross-cutting ID list.
+- [ ] Write the feedback memory file and link from `MEMORY.md`.

 ## Acceptance Criteria

Duplicate `ui.inspect` blocks copy-pasted across six entity YAMLs, redeclared `entity.*` commands inside `task.yaml`/`tag.yaml`/`project.yaml`, and a broken `project.delete` all trace back to this ambiguity.
+Establish the rule that the rest of this refactor enforces. -- **Per-entity schemas** (`swissarmyhammer-kanban/builtin/entities/*.yaml`) list ONLY type-specific command references with overlay metadata (`context_menu`, `keys`). Cross-cutting commands are NOT listed — they auto-emit via the registry flag. If an entity must opt *out* of a cross-cutting command (e.g. attachment does not archive), the Rust `available()` impl on that command handles it.
-

Rust `available()` provides the per-type opt-out (e.g. attachments reject `entity.archive`).
